mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack signals deteriorating consumer spending power and rising recession probability, which forces equity markets to price in lower corporate earnings over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising real sales of automobiles and a declining federal funds rate amplifies consumer credit availability and durable goods demand, producing positive equity returns as corporate revenue expectations rise.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS growth and FEDFUNDS changes yields a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term algorithmic buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month growth fails to predict 5-day forward returns with a Rank IC > 0.01 during post-shock recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate profit growth, which systematically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates, or the out-of-sample R-squared falls below 0.05 across multiple macro cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 195.0, persistent inflation erodes real household purchasing power and forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, generating negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in >65% of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above 195.0, or the threshold effect disappears in a quantile regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts signals synchronized strength in labor income and residential investment, which boosts consumer confidence and generates positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and HOUST yields negative forward returns in >55% of observed periods, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden decline in the unemployment rate during a recessionary trough signals labor market stabilization and impending policy support, triggering short-term equity rebounds over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day forward returns in >60% of recessionary trough periods, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for concurrent monetary policy announcements.
